programmes.show_description: Smoke it out: The team ignites a massive bonfire to investigate the Bubble. But what appears in the smoke is more chilling than anyone expected. (S6, ep 3)
The Hunt For Atlantis: New clues narrow down the possible location of Atlantis and suggest that Plato's mysterious island was the home of an advanced civilisation. (S1, ep 2)
The Top Ten Alien Petroglyphs: Count down the top ten alien petroglyphs around the world, from strange figures to flying craft and carvings of what appear to be astronauts. (S15, ep 15)
Wheeling and Dealing: Tom and Sean try to preserve a buffalo hide while Nancy makes custom moccasins. Martha and Elli transform an old truck into an off-road beast for hunting. (S11, ep 9)
Dark and Dangerous: With just days remaining of python season, Pickle seeks validation from Troy on her tracking skills, while Bruce is forced to hunt alone in Zak's absence. (S3, ep 15)
Fishing For Trouble: Les and Ashley are tempted to take a chance on the world's largest fishing lure, but Seth thinks it will be a tough sell. (S11, ep 2)
Join antiques expert and presenter David Dickinson as more collectors try to sell their items to dealers. Will they strike a deal or take a chance at auction instead? (S9, ep 39)
Leek 4: David Dickinson travels to Leek in Staffordshire, where diamonds are forever and a seven-stone diamond ring causes a stir in the room. (S10, ep 19)
Pawntourage: Things go nuclear when a Japanese Godzilla toy hits the counter. Chum does battle over a jacket and a Johnny Drama whiskey bottle tries to join Chum's entourage. (S24, ep 9)
...Henry's Wreck-trospective. Henry and Sam start at Bristol Classic Car Show, where they acquire a quirky car from the 1970s. (S1, ep 1)
Robot Cowboy: The pressure falls on Darrell and Brandon to drum up merchandise for their website. Plus, Jarrod and Brandi put a bidding strategy to the test. Will it work? (S6, ep 18)
It's Bring Your Kids to Work Day: It's spring break so Jarrod and Brandi bring their kids to the auction. Meanwhile, Darrel puts his 'Gambler' nickname to the test. (S6, ep 19)
Fishing for Trouble: A locker full of fishing gear sparks a fast and furious bidding war that leaves the winner with a surprise catch. (S1, ep 19)
All Out War: The buyers throw down to win a locker with what looks like a hidden tractor. But as the dust settles, one lucky hunter gets the ultimate surprise. (S1, ep 20)
The Big Apple: Containers from New York make their way to Long Beach, California, bidders are interested in a billionaire's unclaimed goods and a one-of-a-kind auction is held. (S2, ep 3)
Eight Tons of Fun: Bidders battle over a super heavyweight freight, while a container filled with outdoor adventure equipment holds a surprise. (S2, ep 4)
Winds of Winter: Jake makes a grisly discovery and Josh rebuilds a windmill to power his cabin. Meanwhile, Kidd and Harry brave raging rapids to retrieve a capsized canoe. (S11, ep 10)
The Final Battle: Alligator season is almost over but there are still lots of gators to catch, not the least the one known as the Loch Ness Monster. Troy faces his nemesis. (S3, ep 16)
Fender Vendor: A woman brings in a Japanese matchlock rifle that weighs almost as much as she does, followed by two classics: a Superman figure and a Tadeo Fender Telecaster. (S24, ep 8)
The Mom Factor: Jarrod and Brandi have some fun as they attempt to beat the competition. Meanwhile, Casey tries her hand at bidding. (S6, ep 20)
The Emperor of El Monte: Buyers Darrell, Brandon, Jarrod, Brandi and Ivy head to El Monte, California where they tackle the local competition head on. (S7, ep 9)
...Henry's Wreck-trospective.The Stratford Wreck- Trospective: Henry's Wreck-trospective dives headfirst into the archives to celebrate the very best bits from the series. (S1, ep 2)
Edgar Cayce - The Sleeping Prophet: In the 1900s, a modern-day prophet Edgar Cayce became a worldwide sensation. He claimed to receive visions of both the past and future. (S15, ep 16)
The Mystery Of Loch Ness: Giorgio Tsoukalos investigates whether sightings of the Loch Ness monster could be caused by a disturbance in the space-time continuum. (S1, ep 4)
Fishing for Trouble: A locker full of fishing gear sparks a fast and furious bidding war that leaves the winner with a surprise catch. (S1, ep 19)
All Out War: The buyers throw down to win a locker with what looks like a hidden tractor. But as the dust settles, one lucky hunter gets the ultimate surprise. (S1, ep 20)
Robot Cowboy: The pressure falls on Darrell and Brandon to drum up merchandise for their website. Plus, Jarrod and Brandi put a bidding strategy to the test. Will it work? (S6, ep 18)
It's Bring Your Kids to Work Day: It's spring break so Jarrod and Brandi bring their kids to the auction. Meanwhile, Darrel puts his 'Gambler' nickname to the test. (S6, ep 19)
Bradford 1: David Dickinson's team are in Bradford. Tim is brimming over a Lalique bowl. Will David Hakeney let a vintage train set go to auction for the sake of a tenner? (S10, ep 20)
Baby on Board!: Two men attempt a carjacking at a Louisiana car wash. A hapless teenage driver turns an Alabama parking lot into a demolition derby. (S1, ep 9)
Wrong Way: A disagreement over driving rules in a Washington State parking lot ends with multiple gunshots. A confrontational driver pushes two friends to the brink. (S1, ep 10)
An Oregon woman doing some work on a remote cabin is spied on by a dark figure. Next, a man in Arkansas encounters something terrifying in the woods. (S8, ep 15)
In Florida, an investigator captures dark shadow figures while walking through a cemetery and a young girl in Ohio sees the ghost of her late grandfather. (S8, ep 16)
Destruction: A poltergeist attaches itself to a sensitive young girl, while a shadow man terrorises a boy and a demonic entity violently attacks a teenager. (S1, ep 5)
Nazi Time Travelers: Experts join the trail of purported Nazi time travel device, Die Glocke. Did it really exist? And could it have been based on alien technology? (S1, ep 3)
Edgar Cayce - The Sleeping Prophet: In the 1900s, a modern-day prophet Edgar Cayce became a worldwide sensation. He claimed to receive visions of both the past and future. (S15, ep 16)
Winds of Winter: Jake makes a grisly discovery and Josh rebuilds a windmill to power his cabin. Meanwhile, Kidd and Harry brave raging rapids to retrieve a capsized canoe. (S11, ep 10)
The Final Battle: Alligator season is almost over but there are still lots of gators to catch, not the least the one known as the Loch Ness Monster. Troy faces his nemesis. (S3, ep 16)
Growing Pains: Les has the opportunity to get his hands on truckloads of brand new goods that could change the store forever. (S11, ep 4)
Join antiques expert and presenter David Dickinson as more collectors try to sell their items to dealers. Will they strike a deal or take a chance at auction instead? (S9, ep 40)
It's the opening week of a new season, and for the fishermen of Gloucester, Massachusetts, the summer ahead promises the chance for big fortunes. (S6, ep 1)
Trials And Tunalations: Every boat except the Wicked Pissah caught fish during the first week, so all eyes are on Captain Paul Herbert to land his first tuna. (S6, ep 2)
Bluefin Brotherhood: It's a long-standing fishing belief that practicing good karma on the ocean brings good in return... but not every captain plays by the rules. (S6, ep 3)
Venom Warriors: Troy, Chase and Bill go deep diving in search of pythons but may have bitten off more than they can chew. Bruce and Zak venture into Viper Jungle. (S2, ep 2)
Skunk Ape Alley: The war against pythons invading the Everglades begins as Troy Landry, his son Chase and snake expert Bill uncover the sepents' breeding grounds. (S2, ep 1)
Return to the Everglades Triangle: The hunters venture into the Everglades Triangle, a remote area known for unexplained disappearances and swarms of snakes. (S2, ep 3)
The Top Ten UFO Crashes: An acorn-shaped crash, a craft in Japan and a baffling impact in Britain - could these strange events prove Earth has been visited? (S17, ep 4)
Mutant Mermaid, UFOs vs Nukes and Seamonster Chases a Boat: Is a dead mermaid really preserved in Japan? And are UFOs attracted to nuclear facilities? (S3, ep 19)
Alien Sabotage, Ghost Child and Hungry Hungry Sasquatch: Strange creatures are encountered and a home security video may be proof that UFOs are messing with technology. (S3, ep 20)
The Bigfoot Files: Tony Harris separates the real from the fake in some of the most compelling Bigfoot evidence recently captured, including huge primate-like footprints. (S4, ep 1)
When satellites capture a massive crater in the picturesque English countryside, historian Martin Morgan heads there to investigate. (S6, ep 12)
A perplexing, intricate symbol high up in the remote Peruvian Andes may be for the grisliest of child sacrifices - death by lightning strike. (S6, ep 13)
When satellites capture a strange oozing grey mass in the Mississippi countryside, scientist Nick Pollock goes to investigate. (S6, ep 14)
Who Is D.B. Cooper?: Delve into the top theories surrounding the case of Dan Cooper, who hijacked a Northwest Orient Airlines flight, before disappearing without a trace. (S4, ep 14)
Unlocking the Secrets of Stonehenge: The mysterious arrangement of stones found in the English countryside remains a mystery as to how - or why - the landmark was built. (S4, ep 15)
Hitler's Holidays of Hate: Experts investigate the methods that Adolf Hitler and the Nazis used to indoctrinate a peaceful population, turning it into a finely-tuned war machine. (S7, ep 1)
Beyond Heaven and Hell: What happens after we die? From heaven and hell to reincarnation, explore the remarkable possibilities of life after death. (S2, ep 1)
Dawn of the Celts: A journey to some of the mysteries of how the early Celts emerged and expanded, from collapsed salt mines, to bizarre rituals and lost cities. (1 of 3)
Mysteries of the Mind: William Shatner explores the abilities of our brains that could one day become science fact, rather than science fiction. (S1, ep 7)
